text: Thinking recently about humans as storytellers, and how most animals have very minimal language. so the way us humans navigate narratives, (ie. navigate "semantic space") is unknowable to other animals, except in the most shallow of ways.

raw.full_text: Thinking recently about humans as storytellers, and how most animals have very minimal language. so the way us humans navigate narratives, (ie. navigate "semantic space") is unknowable to other animals, except in the most shallow of ways.

text:
So we navigate the higher dimensional space of story & mental models with ease, while animals work in lower dimensional worlds of instinct, emotion, physical reality.

So if language & story is our unique thing, what space might another intelligence have the agility to traverse?

text: Humans navigate story to inform models of futures, these models which @drmichaellevin speaks of as illuminated within our cognitive light cones (which animals can't perceive). Story informs our actions, our emotions (which animals CAN perceive & share with us)

raw.full_text: Humans navigate story to inform models of futures, these models which @drmichaellevin speaks of as illuminated within our cognitive light cones (which animals can't perceive). Story informs our actions, our emotions (which animals CAN perceive & share with us)

text: All stories play within a very small value space, underlying the action, with only the smallest bit of value change, often toward a minimal and shared set of human values.

raw.full_text: All stories play within a very small value space, underlying the action, with only the smallest bit of value change, often toward a minimal and shared set of human values.

text: I've been wondering if the thing that WE have limited ability to navigate is value space itself. After all, shared values underpin most human action. Most stories play within a very narrow range. We mostly live and die within a small range of mostly static values...

raw.full_text: I've been wondering if the thing that WE have limited ability to navigate is value space itself. After all, shared values underpin most human action. Most stories play within a very narrow range. We mostly live and die within a small range of mostly static values...

text: But what would an intelligence be like that could move through value space in the same way we move through narrative space? To reach metaphorical "branches" we can't? It would be thoroughly disorienting to us, much as the linguistic space we play in are unfathomable to animals...

text:
It seems that we humans are mostly made of stories, and those stories travel through a very constrained value space...

I wonder if our linguistic versatility makes us like "the only fauna in semantic space", moving nimbly through it, while other animals are like semantic *flora*

raw.full_text: It seems to me that there's a slight element of Lovecraftian horror in this consideration. I don't know lovecraft's work as well as I'd like to, but my sense is that it's about machinations beyond our comprehension. Unknowable value space movements seem scary to me at least...

text: What is the equivalent of a linear "story" being told across value space instead of semantic space? Can we even conceive of a form like that, shifting thru the latent space of values from start to end, as nimbly as we shift through narrative space? (...maybe like corporations?)

text:
Maybe an animal in value space isn't so scary. Maybe it'll appreciate us, like how we admire nature & nature's rich diversity, discovered in its travels thru deep time.

But as for ASI, we should be wary of encountering it too soon, before we truly appreciate how our garden grows
